title: City Transit Funding Talks Resume After Weeks of Budget Gridlock
lead: State and city leaders returned to negotiations on a transit rescue package after service cuts drew opposition from commuters and business groups.
body: Lawmakers reopened negotiations on a funding plan for the regional transit system on Thursday, reviving talks that had stalled over how to split costs between the state and the city.
The transit authority warned earlier this month that, without additional support, it would reduce service frequency on several commuter lines and delay repairs scheduled for the summer.
Business groups and labor unions have both pushed for a deal, arguing that unreliable service is already affecting worker attendance and downtown foot traffic.
Officials involved in the talks said the latest proposal includes a mix of temporary operating support and a longer-term review of fares, tolls, and dedicated tax revenue.
No final agreement was announced, but negotiators said they expect to continue discussions through the weekend.

title: Small Businesses Add Hiring Plans Despite Higher Borrowing Costs
lead: A new survey shows modest confidence among small firms, with owners citing steady consumer demand even as financing remains expensive.
body: Small business owners reported slightly stronger hiring intentions this month, according to a nationwide survey released Thursday.
Respondents said customer demand has remained resilient in food services, personal care, and home maintenance, helping offset pressure from higher interest rates.
At the same time, many firms reported delaying equipment purchases and expansion plans because loans remain costly and cash flow is tighter than a year ago.
Economists said the report points to a mixed environment in which labor demand can stay firm while investment slows.
The survey also found that price increases are moderating, though wage competition remains a challenge in several service industries.

title: Storm Recovery Crews Restore Power to Most Gulf Coast Neighborhoods
lead: Utilities said restoration efforts are nearing completion, though some coastal communities still face flooding and road closures.
body: Utility companies said they have restored electricity to most customers affected by this week's storm system along the Gulf Coast.
Emergency officials cautioned that floodwater remains on roads in low-lying areas, and several school districts announced delayed openings.
Residents in the hardest-hit communities are still waiting for debris removal and inspections before they can return to damaged homes.
State agencies said they are coordinating with local governments on temporary shelter capacity and cleanup contracts.
Weather forecasters expect calmer conditions over the next two days, which could speed up repair work and reopen transportation routes.

title: Regional Summit Opens With Focus on Shipping Security and Food Prices
lead: Leaders from twelve countries began talks on maritime disruptions, inflation pressures, and emergency grain shipments.
body: A regional summit opened Thursday with leaders emphasizing shipping security and the impact of transport disruptions on food prices.
Delegates said they will discuss joint patrol coordination, insurance costs for cargo routes, and contingency planning for agricultural exports.
Several countries arrived with proposals to expand emergency grain reserves after recent delays raised prices in import-dependent markets.
Analysts said the meeting is unlikely to produce a single binding agreement, but could establish common protocols for information sharing.
The summit is scheduled to continue through Friday, with a final statement expected in the evening.

title: Neighborhood Libraries Expand Evening Programs for Adults and Teens
lead: Public libraries are adding workshops, study halls, and language classes as demand rises for low-cost evening activities.
body: Library systems in several cities are expanding evening programming to include career workshops, homework support, and language classes.
Librarians said attendance has grown steadily over the past year, particularly for events designed for teens and working adults.
Some branches are partnering with community colleges and nonprofit groups to provide instructors and digital learning resources.
Officials said the expansion is part of a broader effort to keep libraries accessible as multifunction community spaces.
Program organizers expect to add more sessions this spring if staffing and grant funding remain stable.

title: Late Defensive Stop Seals Playoff Spot in Tight Conference Race
lead: A final-minute block and two free throws helped secure a postseason berth as teams jockey for seeding.
body: A late defensive stop preserved a narrow win Thursday night and clinched a playoff spot for the home team in a crowded conference race.
The game remained close throughout the second half, with both teams trading short runs and frequent lead changes.
Coaches pointed to turnovers and second-chance rebounds as key factors in the closing minutes.
With the result, the standings tighten further around the middle seeds heading into the final stretch of the regular season.
Players said the team will focus on recovery and film study before its next matchup this weekend.
